retry_notes: First generated version established the three-state board but retained too much humanoid-warrior posture and minor pseudo-text-like marks on hanging strips.
Second version tightened non-human body language, suppressed pseudo-writing, kept the existing Suming/Qingming emblem as a small physical plaque, and was accepted as the canonical output.
Face-anthropomorphism revision requested: raise Li Tang's commander facial readability while locking mantis-insect species traits.
First face revision candidate was rejected because it pushed the full body toward a humanoid armored warrior and enlarged the chest emblem beyond the small-plaque rule.

Accepted face revision used the original canonical board as edit target, preserved the non-human insect body plan and small rank plaque, and revised only the head/face planes toward semi-anthropomorphic brow, nose-ridge, cheek-plate, and jaw-armor readability.
results.validation_summary: Final PNG copied to the canonical asset path; earlier canonical version retained as history v002. Visual review confirmed three states, non-human mantis-insect body plan, stronger semi-anthropomorphic command face, dark compound eyes, bone-white mandible guard, black-green carapace, folding forearm blades, reverse-jointed legs, old iron harness, dark-green command belt, bone-white insect-wax strips, snowline state, small locked-emblem rank plaque only, and no readable text.
